data.body: PREMISE CORRECTION (surfaced by the plan-review grounding, verified via git): the '.codex/config.toml' and '.agents/rules/rebar.md' provider-config scaffolding is NOT committed — both are untracked and locally excluded via .git/info/exclude (lines 12, 33) on this one machine; 'git ls-files' returns nothing for them. So the earlier 'repo is ~30% migrated / cross-provider scaffolding already exists' framing was based on untracked working-tree experiments, not committed state. In the committed repo the provider migration is effectively greenfield. Consequence for S6 (acid-pepperish-mare): it now must BRING these files under version control (git add -f + point at AGENTS.md) rather than 'reconcile existing committed files' — otherwise no other clone/provider ever sees them. Default assumption baked into S6: commit them (coherent with the epic goal of guidance *available to* Codex/Cursor/Antigravity); flagged there for override if the team wants provider configs to stay per-developer. This is exactly the ambiguity the de-risking pass was meant to surface.
